# Overcoming resistance: strategies for successful tech adoption in law firms

*This is Part 2 in a series about innovation in law firms. [Click here for Part 1 - Navigating Change: Strategies for Introducing New Technology in Law Firms.](/resources/navigating-change-strategies-for-introducing-new-technology-in-law-firms/)*

Introducing new technology in [law firms](/use-cases/industries/law-and-conveyancing/) often meets with resistance, particularly from partners and senior lawyers who are accustomed to established workflows. Once you’ve effectively communicated the need for change in the first place, the next step is to address the underlying concerns and barriers to adoption, ensuring a smoother transition and greater acceptance of the new technology.   
  
This article takes a look at what you need to know to overcome resistance for implementing new tech in law firms.

#### 1. Understand their drivers

Lawyers are a particularly difficult group to sell to – they often need to see an individual or personal benefit to them. To achieve a successful implementation then, it’s critical to see and understand each partner’s particular driver (e.g. maintaining the relationship, or speed of opening the file or doing a particular task), rather than simply selling the vision.

Without buy-in, they will simply find alternatives, such as doing things manually or getting a junior or an assistant to complete the tasks.

#### 2. Deal with tech laggards through training and support

Fortunately, this is fading with COVID, as employees and partners alike have had to get up to speed with collaboration tools such as Teams, Zoom, and the like. In order to deal with this group, Catriona suggests a divide and conquer approach.

“It’s useful to think of where people are on the change cycle and plot the pilot group to that," says Catriona. “You can then mix up pilot groups and tailor messages to participants.”

Enthusiasts can be brought in early and trained up so they can evangelise and model good adoption, but you don’t just want to focus on the positive people, you need people with a variety of views taking part in a pilot.

Meanwhile, empathy can go a long way. “No one wants to feel like they’re not getting it," Catriona advises. Carving out different groups of users – whether by seniority or use case – so they can ask ‘stupid’ questions without wanting to look bad in front of peers or bosses can also be useful.

“Resistance can often be put down to people just not being familiar with, or knowing what to do next," says Catriona.

#### 3. Deal with the loss of control

Often process changes may take some level of visibility away from fee-earners with non-revenue generating activity being handed off to centralised teams or legal assistants.

Understanding ways that they can ‘check in on’ or get visibility can be useful so that they can see where it is without them doing the grunt work.

#### 4. Allow for exceptions and alternative processes

There can be some resistance in teams because of the nature of the clients – for instance, with private or HNW clients.

Working with the partners to understand their concerns, and finding alternate ways to fit the ‘new standard’, can be beneficial.

#### 5. Give visibility to the initiative

Generating visibility and excitement around new tech initiatives is key for driving adoption. For instance, when Weightmans rolled out bAIley, a chatbot for internal knowledge sharing, they built ‘buzz’ by doing an office roadshow featuring a 6-foot cut-out of bAIley.

"Having that physical presence rotating through our locations really helped raise the chatbot's visibility,” says Catriona.

Simple, creative promotions that make initiatives tangible can go a long way in getting partners and staff interested and ready to adopt the change.

#### 6. Beware of scope creep

When looking to onboard a new piece of tech, be aware of changes in scope or starting with a project that is too large i.e boiling the ocean. Particularly with product or innovation teams, Catriona warns, you “need to be conscious that you’re a cost centre – you need to show value.”

Despite the impulse to advocate for broad change, it’s necessary to break projects down into chunks that can then show value/ROI.

#### 7. Be aware of history

What’s more, when tech projects have gone unsuccessfully in the past, teams can face headwinds or a lack of morale around implementing new solutions.

Keeping user trust by providing guardrails around what can and can’t be used (for instance, how emerging technology can and can’t be used by fee-earners) can help deal with the sentiment that all technology is bad, and find a middle ground for reasonable use cases.

### Conclusion

Overcoming resistance to new technology in law firms requires a thoughtful and strategic approach.

By understanding the individual drivers of partners, providing targeted training and support, and creating visibility and excitement around new initiatives, firms can navigate the challenges of tech adoption.

Ultimately, with empathy and effective communication, you can navigate your way through your next tech implementation project and position your firm for long term success.  
  
*This is Part 2 in a series about innovation in law firms. [Click here for Part 1 - Navigating Change: Strategies for Introducing New Technology in Law Firms.](/resources/navigating-change-strategies-for-introducing-new-technology-in-law-firms/)*

# Legal Tech Sales Strategies

Find out what AI engines know about you with our AI brand monitor.

The global legal technology market, often referred to as [LegalTech](https://www.insivia.com/expertise/legaltech/), is a rapidly expanding sector of the technology industry. As of 2021, the revenue of the LegalTech market amounted to US$ 27.6 Billion and it is projected to reach an impressive [US$ 69.7 Billion by 2032](https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/legaltech-market), growing at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 8.9%. This substantial growth is a testament to the increasing acceptance and integration of technology within the legal industry.

Legal tech includes tools, platforms, and services that streamline legal processes like research, document management, contract analysis, litigation support, and compliance. The goal is to increase efficiency, reduce costs, and improve legal service delivery. [Key companies in this space](https://ascendixtech.com/legal-tech-overview-best-legal-tech-companies/) are Ascendix Technologies, Brightflag, Docket Alarm, Lexoo, and Luminance.

However, penetrating the LegalTech market and achieving sales success can be challenging due to industry complexities. Effective sales strategies are crucial. In LegalTech, a successful strategy goes beyond selling products/services; it considers legal professionals’ unique needs, pain points, and expectations. Understanding the legal sector, building trust, and showcasing how a LegalTech solution can add value to operations are important.

[A strong sales strategy](https://www.insivia.com/interactive-sales-enablement/) in LegalTech leads to increased market share, higher customer retention, and business growth. In the following sections, we’ll explore key aspects of LegalTech sales strategies: technology, value addition, data-driven approaches, and more.

![Legal tech statistics](https://www.insivia.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Group-31.png)  
Source: [Artificial Lawyer](https://www.artificiallawyer.com/2022/01/26/legal-market-to-hit-1-2-trillion-by-2028-good-news-for-legal-tech/)

![Legal tech statistics](https://www.insivia.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Group-31.png)

In this article, we will discuss the key sales strategies that legal tech software companies can use to succeed in this competitive market.

## **Understanding the Legal Tech Market**

In order to devise an effective sales strategy in the LegalTech sector, it is crucial to first gain a comprehensive understanding of the market. This involves defining your target audience, identifying common customer pain points and challenges, and recognizing the importance of trust and relationship building.

## **Defining the Target Audience in the Legal Tech Market**

LegalTech market includes diverse customers like law firms, corporate legal departments, independent lawyers, legal aid organizations, and government agencies. Each group has unique needs and preferences, so it’s crucial to consider them when designing and marketing a LegalTech solution.

For example, large law firms may prioritize solutions that handle big data and integrate smoothly with existing systems. Independent lawyers, on the other hand, may prefer affordable and user-friendly tools that automate tasks and boost productivity. To tailor your sales strategy, start by identifying and understanding your target audience’s specific needs.

### **Common Customer Pain Points and Challenges in Legal Tech Adoption**

LegalTech offers clear benefits, its adoption can come with challenges. The learning curve for new technologies is a common hurdle, especially for non-tech-savvy legal professionals. Security and compliance also pose concerns due to sensitive information and stringent regulations. Additionally, cost is an issue, with the initial investment being substantial for smaller firms or independent practitioners. Nonetheless, LegalTech can lead to significant savings in time and resources.

### **Importance of Trust and Relationship Building in the Legal Tech Market**

Trust is vital in the legal profession. Legal professionals handle sensitive information and make decisions with significant implications for clients. They require reliable, secure, and efficient technology. Building trust involves understanding the legal profession’s unique challenges, providing excellent customer service, and offering robust support and training. Strong customer relationships yield valuable insights to refine products and sales strategies. By being a reliable partner, long-term loyalty is fostered. Understanding the LegalTech market entails knowing customers’ challenges and needs and building trust with them. With this understanding, a sales strategy that positions you as a trusted partner can be developed.

## **Role of Technology in Legal Tech Sales**

The LegalTech industry thrives on innovation and providing advanced solutions to legal professionals. It also leverages technology to enhance sales strategies. The evolving technological landscape is reshaping how LegalTech companies approach sales.

### **How Technology is Reshaping Sales Strategies in the Legal Tech Industry**

Technology is crucial for modernizing sales strategies in the LegalTech industry. It transforms traditional processes into efficient, data-driven, and customer-centric practices. Gathering, analyzing, and interpreting data helps LegalTech companies understand customers, forecast trends, and make informed decisions.

Additionally, technology automates tasks, freeing up time for strategic initiatives like building customer relationships and closing deals. Digital communication channels revolutionize the way LegalTech companies interact with customers, making it easier to reach out, respond, and provide personalized service.

### **Specific Technologies Enhancing Sales Efforts**

Several specific technologies are playing a transformative role in LegalTech sales.

**Customer Relationship Management (CRM)** systems, for instance, are invaluable tools for managing customer interactions. They help sales teams track leads, manage contacts, schedule follow-ups, and maintain a comprehensive record of all customer interactions.

This information can be used to personalize communications, anticipate customer needs, and build stronger relationships.

**Artificial Intelligence (AI)** is another game-changer in LegalTech sales. AI-powered chatbots, for example, can handle initial customer inquiries, providing instant responses and freeing up sales reps to focus on more complex tasks.

AI can also be used to analyze sales data, identify patterns and trends, and generate actionable insights to inform sales strategies.

**Data Analytics** tools are essential for making sense of the vast amounts of data generated by digital interactions. They enable companies to monitor and analyze various metrics, such as website traffic, click-through rates, conversion rates, and customer behavior patterns. These insights can be used to refine sales strategies, improve customer targeting, and optimize marketing efforts.

In the LegalTech industry, technology is not just a product but also a vital enabler of effective sales strategies. By utilizing tools like CRM systems, AI, and data analytics, LegalTech companies can boost sales, offer superior customer service, and stay ahead in a competitive market.

## **Value Addition in Legal Tech Sales**

In the realm of Legal Tech sales, adding value exceeds innovative products and services. It includes educational content, personalized experiences, and credible testimonials to boost sales efforts.

### **The Significance of Educational Content in Legal Tech Sales**

Educational content plays a crucial role in LegalTech sales, bridging the knowledge gap among potential customers. It demystifies complex technologies, making them accessible and appealing to legal professionals.

Content can take various forms like blog posts, whitepapers, webinars, and learning modules. These resources explain features, provide practical guidance, and highlight industry trends.

By offering insights and practical knowledge, educational content establishes a company as an authority, fostering trust among customers. It helps them understand how a solution addresses their challenges, increasing the likelihood of a purchase.

### **Custom Content Automation for Personalized Client Experiences**

Custom content automation is an effective strategy for adding value in LegalTech sales. It involves using technology to deliver tailored content to each potential customer based on their unique needs, preferences, and behavior patterns.

For example, a law firm in need of a document management system would receive content highlighting the efficiency and security benefits of a specific solution.

By providing personalized content, companies can engage potential customers more effectively and demonstrate an understanding of their needs while presenting their products or services in a relevant and compelling way.

### **Case Studies and Testimonials as a Value Addition**

Case studies and testimonials are potent tools for establishing credibility and showcasing the real-world impact of LegalTech solutions. These resources offer tangible evidence of how products or services have benefited other customers, making it easier for potential buyers to visualize the potential benefits.

Case studies provide detailed accounts of customer implementation, challenges, and results, while testimonials feature brief but impactful statements from satisfied customers praising quality, performance, or value. Both can strengthen sales efforts by leveraging social proof, where potential customers gain reassurance from seeing others engage with a reliable, effective, and valuable solution.

In summary, LegalTech sales involve more than just selling a product. It’s about educating customers, personalizing experiences, and providing credible evidence of success. By adopting these strategies, LegalTech companies can enhance their sales and build stronger customer relationships.

## **Data-Driven Sales Strategies**

In the era of digital transformation, data has emerged as a valuable asset that can drive significant competitive advantage. In the context of LegalTech sales, data analytics plays an essential role in decoding customer behavior and preferences, thereby refining and optimizing sales strategies.

### **The Role of Data Analytics in Understanding Customer Behavior and Preferences**

Customer behavior understanding is crucial in LegalTech for effective sales strategies. Data analytics becomes a strategic ally, involving specialized systems and software to analyze vast amounts of raw data. By scrutinizing website visits, social media interactions, email responses, and more, data analytics reveals patterns and trends in customer behavior. This provides invaluable insights into customer preferences, decision-making processes, and purchasing factors.

Moreover, data analytics unveils preferred communication channels, content engagement, and valued features in LegalTech solutions. Armed with these insights, LegalTech companies can tailor sales strategies to meet specific customer needs, enhancing satisfaction and boosting sales.

### **How Data Can Help Refine and Optimize Sales Strategies in Legal Tech**

Data driven decision-making is transforming LegalTech sales. Leveraging sales data, companies can identify effective strategies and optimize them. For instance, if a marketing campaign generates leads but low conversions, adjustments may be needed to drive purchases.

Analyzing customer interactions can reveal common queries, allowing the creation of helpful content. Data also aids in forecasting sales trends, anticipating customer needs, and staying competitive.

Through continuous data monitoring and analysis, LegalTech companies can make informed decisions, adapt strategies, and drive growth. A data-driven approach enhances understanding, tailors experiences, and keeps companies ahead in the market.

## **Customer Support in Legal Tech Sales**

Customer support is a critical component of the sales process, particularly in the LegalTech industry where solutions can be complex and highly specialized. The level of customer service provided can significantly impact sales, shaping the customer’s experience and influencing their purchasing decision.

### **The Impact of Excellent Customer Service on Legal Tech Sales**

Exemplary customer service has a significant impact on LegalTech sales. It sets companies apart in a competitive market, fostering loyalty, satisfaction, and driving growth.

Firstly, excellent customer service builds trust with potential customers by promptly addressing queries and effectively resolving issues. This makes customers feel valued and confident in the company’s support.

Moreover, outstanding customer service aids in retention. Retaining existing customers is often more cost-effective than acquiring new ones. Exceptional support increases customer satisfaction, leading to higher retention rates and recurring revenue.

Finally, excellent customer service generates positive word-of-mouth referrals, a powerful sales tool. Satisfied customers are likely to recommend the company, attracting new business without further marketing.

### **Strategies to Enhance Customer Support in the Legal Tech Industry**

Improving customer support in the LegalTech industry involves a combination of strategic initiatives, innovative technology, and a customer-centric mindset.

**Implementing a robust support system:** This could include a dedicated support team, a comprehensive FAQ section on the website, and clear channels of communication (like email, phone, and live chat). A multi-channel support system ensures that customers can reach out via the channel they are most comfortable with3.

**Leveraging technology:** Tools like CRM systems can help manage customer interactions effectively. AI-powered chatbots can provide instant responses to common queries, freeing up support staff to handle more complex issues4.

**Providing ongoing training to support staff:** Given the technical nature of LegalTech solutions, it’s crucial for support staff to stay abreast of product updates, new features, and common issues. Regular training can ensure they are equipped to provide accurate and helpful support.

**Listening to customer feedback:** Customer feedback is an invaluable resource for improving support services. Regular surveys and feedback sessions can help identify areas for improvement and monitor the effectiveness of [current support strategies](https://www.helpscout.com/helpu/customer-service-tips/).

In conclusion, customer support plays a pivotal role in LegalTech sales. By providing excellent service, companies can build trust, retain customers, and drive sales growth. Furthermore, by implementing strategic initiatives and leveraging technology, they can enhance their support services and deliver superior customer experiences.

## **Go-To-Market Strategy for Legal Tech**

In the competitive landscape of LegalTech, a well-defined go-to-market (GTM) strategy is not just a nicety—it’s a necessity. A GTM strategy outlines the way in which a company introduces its product to the market, encompassing everything from identifying target customers to defining sales and marketing approaches.

### **Importance of a Well-Defined Go-To-Market Strategy**

A strong GTM strategy drives market penetration, enhances customer engagement, and accelerates growth. It aligns all aspects of a business towards market success.

In the LegalTech industry, a well-defined GTM strategy helps companies stand out, communicate their value, and build relationships. It enables companies to anticipate trends, respond to customer needs, and stay ahead of the competition.

A GTM strategy facilitates decision-making, optimizes resource allocation, and drives efficiency. It enhances accountability, allowing companies to monitor progress and adjust tactics as needed.

### **Key Elements of an Effective Go-To-Market Strategy in the Legal Tech Industry**

An effective GTM strategy in the LegalTech industry should encompass several key elements:

**Market Segmentation:** Identifying the target audience is the first step in any GTM strategy. In the LegalTech context, this could involve segmenting the market based on firm size, practice area, or specific legal challenges.

**Value Proposition:** The value proposition articulates why a law firm or legal department should choose a particular LegalTech solution over others. It should clearly highlight the unique benefits and advantages of the product.

**Sales and Marketing Strategy:** This involves determining the most effective channels to reach target customers—be it through direct sales, online advertising, content marketing, social media, or industry events.

**Customer Support:** As discussed earlier, excellent customer service can significantly impact sales in the LegalTech industry. A comprehensive customer support plan should be an integral part of any GTM strategy.

**Pricing Model:** The pricing model should reflect the value provided by the product and be competitive within the market. It should also take into account the budget constraints and preferences of target customers

A well-defined GTM strategy is key for success in the LegalTech market. By focusing on market segmentation, value proposition, sales and marketing strategy, customer support, and pricing model, companies effectively introduce products, engage customers, and drive growth.

## **Avoiding Common Pitfalls in Legal Tech Sales**

In the fast-paced world of LegalTech, sales success is about implementing the right strategies and avoiding common pitfalls. By recognizing these missteps as opportunities for improvement, LegalTech companies can achieve sales success with confidence.

### **Tactics that Legal Tech Companies Should Avoid**

**Neglecting customer needs:** In the quest to innovate, some LegalTech companies may lose sight of the most critical element—the customer. Developing a solution without a clear understanding of the customer’s needs and pain points can result in a product that fails to resonate with the target audience.

**Overcomplicating the sales process:** LegalTech solutions can be complex, but the sales process shouldn’t be. A complicated sales process can overwhelm potential customers and deter them from making a purchase.

**Undervaluing customer support:** As discussed earlier, customer support plays a pivotal role in LegalTech sales. Failing to provide prompt, effective customer service can erode trust, diminish customer satisfaction, and ultimately impact sales.

**Ignoring feedback:** Customer feedback is an invaluable resource for improving products and services. Ignoring this feedback can lead to missed opportunities for enhancement and growth.

### **Turning Common Pitfalls into Opportunities for Improvement**

**Focusing on the customer:** By placing the customer at the center of all decisions, LegalTech companies can ensure their solutions align with the needs and expectations of their target audience. This customer-centric approach can drive product development, shape marketing strategies, and guide sales efforts.

**Simplifying the sales process:** Streamlining the sales process can enhance the customer experience, making it easier for potential customers to understand the value of the solution and make a purchasing decision. This could involve simplifying pricing models, providing clear product information, and offering personalized demonstrations.

**Investing in customer support:** By prioritizing customer service, companies can build stronger relationships with their customers, enhancing satisfaction and loyalty. This could involve investing in training for support staff, implementing a robust support system, and leveraging technology to improve response times.

**Leveraging feedback:** By actively seeking and acting on customer feedback, companies can continually refine their products and services. This feedback-driven approach can lead to product enhancements, improved customer service, and innovative solutions that meet the evolving needs of the legal industry4.

Avoiding common pitfalls in LegalTech sales involves a combination of strategic foresight, customer focus, operational simplicity, and continuous improvement. By transforming these pitfalls into opportunities for growth, LegalTech companies can navigate the path to sales success with greater confidence and efficacy.

=========================================

Insights

Litigation, Software

![Image 2: Opus 2](https://www.opus2.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/Profile-logo-gradient.png)

Opus 2|1 Aug 2024

![Image 3: Legal tech adoption image for Opus 2 Blog Legal Case Management Software Adoption](https://www.opus2.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Legal-tech-user-adoption-tips-1024x535.jpg)

In today’s competitive and innovative litigation landscape, legal tech adoption is pivotal for law firms as they work to meet their clients’ evolving needs and develop stronger client relationships. However, despite significant investments in promising tools, many litigation teams struggle with low adoption rates and inconsistent use across departments and teams.

Understanding the barriers legal tech adoption is crucial to overcoming these obstacles. This blog explores the common challenges hindering the successful implementation of legal technology and provides seven practical tips to facilitate smoother adoption within your firm and litigation department.

Unmet legal tech expectations
-----------------------------

The vast majority of lawyers responding to a [recent Wolters Kluwer survey](https://www.wolterskluwer.com/en/know/future-ready-lawyer-2023)—85 percent of lawyers in law firms say they expect to make greater use of legal technology to improve their future productivity. But less than half (46%) believe they are fully leveraging legal technology in their daily work today.

Why are expectations for legal tech so far removed from reality?

Often, the problem is poor adoption. While many law firms have eagerly invested in new tools, excited about their potential to make legal work and client service easier and more efficient, continued investments are at risk due to “shelfware”—unused and forgotten technology that never delivers on its promised ROI.Let’s dig into why.

4 common legal tech adoption challenges
---------------------------------------

The first step in promoting better adoption of legal tech is understanding why adoption so often fails. Here are four common challenges.

### 1. Old habits die hard

Attorneys’ resistance to change is a significant obstacle to adopting new technologies. The law is based on precedent, so it’s no wonder that lawyers tend to rely on tried-and-true [legal case management workflows](https://www.opus2.com/en-us/legal-case-management-workflow/) that have served them well for years. Indeed, the Wolters Kluwer survey said attorneys remaining stuck in familiar ways and slow decision-making are the biggest barriers (44%) to investment in legal technology.

### 2. Unclear benefits to users

Legal professionals need to understand how a particular tool will make their jobs easier, more efficient, or more effective. If they can’t picture or understand the benefits, new technology simply feels like a burden. And, without this clarity, the motivation to change existing habits is minimal.

### 3. Time required to learn

About a third of attorneys (36%) in the Wolters Kluwer survey said they lack the requisite skills, knowledge, or understanding of legal technology. But time is a precious commodity for litigation lawyers; few want set aside dedicated time to learn how to use new software, especially when its benefits aren’t immediately apparent.

### 4. Lack of incentives or accountability

What incentive is there for attorneys to adopt or engage with new technology? And, if senior leaders don’t make it a priority, why would lawyers who prefer their existing (inefficient) processes embrace new technology? Without incentives or accountability, lawyers who don’t feel personally responsible for driving adoption are unlikely to participate. Often, this leaves technology initiatives to fall by the wayside.

tips

7 practical tips to encourage legal tech adoption
-------------------------------------------------

Investing in technology—even when the chosen tool seems like a perfect fit for your team—doesn’t automatically compel adoption. To ease the process of legal tech implementation, you have to build a case for change and lay the foundation. Keep these seven tips in mind.

### 1. Research to find the right tool for your team

Boosting adoption of [legacy legal case management software](https://www.opus2.com/en-us/5-benefits-of-upgrading-your-case-management-software/) won’t do you any good if it doesn’t match your organization’s current and future needs. The legal technology market moves quickly but many legacy case management systems haven’t offered substantive updates in years. So, it may be time to go shopping and explore [best-in-class case solutions.](https://www.opus2.com/en-us/solutions/cases/)

But, before you buy, talk with lawyers and litigation support to find out what real-world challenges they’re facing. Which processes are most time consuming? Which are most stressful? Look for intuitive, easy-to-use solutions that are purpose-built for the entire litigation lifecycle, minimizing the learning curve and maximizing usability. A user-centered design approach ensures that the technology integrates seamlessly into existing workflows and enhances the overall user experience.

### 2. Customize the solution to meet your needs

One-size-fits-all solutions rarely work well in the legal field. Identify the key processes that your lawyers are following and break them down into distinct processes, tasks, and workflows. Map each process to visualize its workflow, identifying bottlenecks and inefficiencies.

Then look for solutions that address these pain points and integrate with your existing solutions. Finally, work with the legal tech provider to customize those solutions, making the workflows, dashboards, and reporting outputs mesh with your current operations. That will help your team readily fit the new tool into their daily routine–this is particularly important when considering the value of AI-enabled software.

### 3. Be proactive with change management

Effective change management minimizes disruption and builds confidence in the new system. Assemble a change management team with representatives from each group of users, including attorneys, paralegals, administrative staff, and IT personnel, to solicit diverse perspectives and ensure buy-in. Identify potential challenges for each group, such as resistance to change, skill gaps, and workflow disruptions. Then, define a roadmap for the transition.

Work with your solution provider to tailor their onboarding process to your users’ needs, creating clear timelines and support mechanisms to keep the process on track. Set measurable objectives and key milestones for the change management process, such as achieving 70 percent user adoption within six months.

### 4. Focus on pain points, user benefits, and client value

When introducing new technology, highlight how it will address the specific pain points that your team is experiencing. Demonstrating how the tool directly benefits users—and provides more value to clients—can significantly enhance buy-in from your team. Get people excited about the tech by using real-world examples to emphasize how it will improve their efficiency, productivity, accuracy, and client service.

### 5. Offer a variety of training options and resources

Everyone learns in their own way. Provide a variety of training options such as in-person workshops, interactive demonstrations, online tutorials, and user manuals to accommodate those varied learning styles. You may also want to designate peer mentors to facilitate knowledge sharing and support among your team members and offer ongoing learning opportunities, such as webinars and refresher courses.

### 6. Ask for feedback and highlight power users

After implementing new technology, regularly seek feedback from users to identify areas for improvement and better alignment with user needs and preferences. Deploy regular surveys and questionnaires to gather structured feedback on usability, functionality, and overall satisfaction. Collect more in-depth, qualitative feedback by organizing focus group sessions where a diverse group of users can discuss their experiences and brainstorm potential solutions.

Leverage power users—those who have successfully maximized their usage of the technology—as a source of knowledge and inspiration. Power users often become the best technology advocates and innovators, influencing their peers and providing informal support and training to their colleagues.

### 7. Look for opportunities to optimize your technology

Post-legal tech implementation, continuously evaluate the technology’s performance and impact to optimize the value of your investment. Conduct regular performance evaluations to assess how well the technology is meeting its intended goals.

Gather quantitative metrics such as time saved and costs reduced as well as qualitative user feedback about overall efficiency and productivity. Monitor usage statistics to see how frequently and effectively the technology is being used and to detect any underused functionalities. Do your users believe the tech is delivering the expected value? Use this information to identify gaps between the technology’s performance and its potential, then implement any identified improvements and reasonable customizations.

Note that many [cloud-based legal case management solutions](https://www.opus2.com/en-us/cloud-based-legal-case-management-software/) offer collaborative development processes based on user feedback; stay in touch with the provider to make the most of these opportunities.

You don’t**just** need legal tech—you need better legal tech implementation
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Legal technology is critical to staying productive and competitive in the market. Ultimately, success lies in transforming your legal team’s approach to truly implement your legal technology and use it to deliver better service and outcomes for clients. When you invest time and effort into the adoption process, you can achieve long-term efficiency gains and build a more innovative, responsive legal practice.
